# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official NodeJS runtime as a parent image
FROM node:current-slim

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install any needed packages specified in package.json
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install

# Copy source code into Docker container at specified path
COPY . .

# Make port 80 available outside of this container to the host machine
EXPOSE 80

# Define environment variable
ENV NAME World

# Run app.js when the container launches
CMD [ "node", "app.js" ]
